issues.title: No baseline vs. actual comparison — schedule is blind to slippage
issues.description: src/api/endpoints/schedule.ts returns only current phase data with no baseline_start_date, baseline_end_date, or baseline_percent_complete columns. The Gantt in src/components/schedule/GanttChart.tsx cannot render gray baseline bars behind actual bars. Without baseline vs. actual, PMs cannot perform Time Impact Analysis, which is a core legal and contractual requirement for every GC.
issues.prompt:
FILE: src/api/endpoints/schedule.ts
FILE: src/components/schedule/GanttChart.tsx
FILE: src/types/api.ts (SchedulePhaseRow)

TASK: Add baseline schedule comparison to the scheduling module.

CHANGES:
1. In SchedulePhaseRow (src/types/api.ts), add fields: baseline_start_date: string | null, baseline_end_date: string | null, baseline_percent_complete: number | null, float_days: number | null, is_critical_path: boolean.
2. In src/api/endpoints/schedule.ts getSchedulePhases(), map these new fields: baselineStartDate, baselineEndDate, baselineProgress, floatDays, critical.
3. In src/components/schedule/GanttChart.tsx, render a secondary gray bar (opacity 0.35, bg #9CA3AF, same row, height 8px) behind the actual progress bar using baselineStartDate/baselineEndDate offsets. Add a legend: 'Baseline' (gray) vs 'Actual' (orange).
4. Add a toggle button 'Show Baseline' that hides/shows baseline bars.
5. If baseline dates are null, show a yellow banner: 'No baseline set — click Set Baseline to snapshot current schedule.'

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Baseline bars render correctly on all phases where baseline data exists.
- Toggle works without remount.
- Float days are displayed as a numeric badge on each row.
- No TypeScript errors. No any casts introduced.
- Phases with float_days = 0 and is_critical_path = true render with a red left border.

issues.title: No AI predictive delay engine — the core north-star feature is absent
issues.description: The schedule module has zero AI integration. src/lib/predictions.ts exists but is never called from src/pages/Schedule.tsx or the Gantt component. There is no Monday-morning prediction panel telling the PM what will go wrong by Friday. This is the single most differentiating feature vs. Procore and its absence makes the schedule module indistinguishable from legacy tools.
issues.prompt:
FILE: src/lib/predictions.ts
FILE: src/pages/Schedule.tsx
FILE: src/components/schedule/GanttChart.tsx
FILE: src/stores/scheduleStore.ts

TASK: Wire the predictive delay engine into the Schedule page.

CHANGES:
1. In src/lib/predictions.ts, export a function predictScheduleRisks(phases: MappedPhase[], weatherForecast: WeatherDay[]): PredictedRisk[] that returns risks with fields: phaseId, title, likelihoodPercent, impactDays, reason, suggestedAction.
2. In src/pages/Schedule.tsx, call predictScheduleRisks after phases and weather load. Store results in local state.
3. Render an AI Risk Panel above the Gantt (collapsible, default open). Each risk card shows: orange warning icon, phase name, likelihood badge (e.g., '78% likely'), impact ('+3 days'), reason sentence, and a 'View Recovery Plan' button that opens the AI Copilot panel with pre-filled prompt.
4. On each Gantt row with a predicted risk, render a small orange lightning bolt icon that opens a tooltip with the risk detail.
5. Add a 'Last analyzed: X minutes ago' timestamp with a 'Re-analyze' button.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Panel renders with at least mock risk data when predictions return results.
- Empty state: 'No risks detected for the next 7 days. Schedule looks healthy.' with green check icon.
- Re-analyze triggers re-fetch and shows a loading skeleton.
- Copilot panel pre-fill works end-to-end.
- No TypeScript errors.

issues.title: Metric cards show no calculated schedule KPIs — all placeholders
issues.description: src/pages/Schedule.tsx renders no metric card row at the top of the page (violating the mandatory design standard that every page starts with 3–5 metric cards). There are no live calculations for schedule variance in days, SPI (Schedule Performance Index), critical path length, float consumed, or activities completed on time. A PM has no at-a-glance health signal when opening the schedule.
issues.prompt:
FILE: src/pages/Schedule.tsx
FILE: src/api/endpoints/schedule.ts
FILE: src/lib/criticalPath.ts

TASK: Add a calculated metric card row to the Schedule page.

CHANGES:
1. In src/lib/criticalPath.ts, export computeScheduleKPIs(phases: MappedPhase[]): ScheduleKPIs returning: { scheduleVarianceDays: number, spi: number, criticalPathLength: number, floatConsumedPct: number, activitiesOnTimePct: number, projectedCompletionDate: string }.
 - SPI = sum(earned_progress * planned_duration) / sum(planned_duration * expected_progress_by_today).
 - Schedule variance = today vs. baseline_end of last critical-path phase.
2. In src/pages/Schedule.tsx, call computeScheduleKPIs after phases load. Render 5 metric cards in a horizontal row using the design token card style (white bg, 12px border-radius, 24px padding, 1px #E5E7EB border):
 - Schedule Variance (days, red if negative, green if 0+)
 - SPI (numeric, color-coded: green ≥1.0, amber 0.85-0.99, red <0.85)
 - Critical Path (N activities)
 - Float Consumed (%)
 - Projected Completion (date string, color vs. baseline)
3. Each card: 24px icon (Lucide), 12px muted label, 28px bold value, 12px trend arrow.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- All 5 cards render with real calculated values from phase data.
- Color coding matches design standard.
- Cards reflow to 2-column grid at mobile breakpoint.
- Skeleton loaders shown while data loads.
- No hardcoded numbers.

issues.title: Gantt chart has no drag-to-adjust, no dependency arrows, no milestone markers
issues.description: src/components/schedule/GanttChart.tsx lacks interactive drag handles for adjusting activity dates, has no visual dependency arrows between linked activities (Finish-to-Start, Start-to-Start), and no milestone diamond markers. Procore and MS Project both support these. Without them the Gantt is a read-only chart, not a planning tool — a critical gap for PMs who need to model recovery plans.
issues.prompt:
FILE: src/components/schedule/GanttChart.tsx
FILE: src/types/api.ts (SchedulePhaseRow)

TASK: Add interactive drag-to-adjust, dependency arrows, and milestone markers to GanttChart.

CHANGES:
1. Add predecessor_ids: string[] to SchedulePhaseRow. In GanttChart, render SVG lines between dependent bars: line from right edge of predecessor bar to left edge of successor bar, using a cubic bezier, stroke #6B7280, strokeWidth 1.5, arrowhead marker.
2. Add drag handles (6px wide, darker shade of bar color) at left and right edges of each bar. On mousedown/touchstart, enter drag mode: update startDate/endDate in local state with snapping to day boundaries. On drop, call onPhaseUpdate(id, { start_date, end_date }) callback prop.
3. For phases where is_milestone=true, render a diamond shape (rotate(45deg) square, 14px, orange fill) at the start date instead of a bar.
4. Add keyboard support: when a bar is focused (Tab), Left/Right arrows shift dates by 1 day, Shift+arrows by 1 week.
5. Unsaved changes: show a yellow 'Unsaved changes' banner with Save and Discard buttons.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Drag works on both mouse and touch (mobile).
- Dependency arrows update position live during drag.
- Milestone diamonds render correctly.
- Keyboard date-shifting works and announces changes via aria-live.
- onPhaseUpdate is called with correct ISO date strings on drop/keyboard confirm.

issues.title: 3-week look-ahead in LookaheadBoard has no weather overlay or trade conflict detection
issues.description: src/components/schedule/LookaheadBoard.tsx renders upcoming activities but does not cross-reference the weather forecast API (src/lib/weather.ts) to flag outdoor activities at risk from rain or extreme temperatures, and does not detect two trades scheduled in the same location on the same day. Both are standard look-ahead functions used by every competent superintendent.
issues.prompt:
FILE: src/components/schedule/LookaheadBoard.tsx
FILE: src/lib/weather.ts
FILE: src/pages/Lookahead.tsx

TASK: Add weather overlay and trade conflict detection to the 3-week look-ahead.

CHANGES:
1. In LookaheadBoard.tsx, accept a weatherForecast: WeatherDay[] prop. For each day column, if precipitation probability > 50% or temperature < 32°F or > 100°F, render a colored weather badge on the column header (blue raindrop for rain, red thermometer for heat, snowflake for freeze).
2. For each activity card on a weather-flagged day, if the activity has work_type includes 'outdoor' or 'concrete' or 'roofing', add an amber warning badge '⚠ Weather Risk' with a tooltip showing the forecast.
3. Implement trade conflict detection: group activities by day and location. If two activities share the same location field on the same day, render a red 'Trade Conflict' badge on both cards with tooltip listing the conflicting trades.
4. Add a filter pill row above the board: 'All', 'Weather Risks', 'Trade Conflicts', 'Critical Path Only'.
5. In src/pages/Lookahead.tsx, fetch weather via getWeatherForecast(projectId, 21) and pass to LookaheadBoard.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Weather badges appear on correct day columns.
- Trade conflict detection works for same location + same day.
- Filter pills hide non-matching activities.
- Weather API errors are caught; board renders without forecast if API fails.
- All new props are fully typed with no any.

issues.title: Schedule page is desktop-only — no mobile-optimized view for superintendent
issues.description: src/pages/Schedule.tsx renders the full Gantt chart without any responsive fallback. On a 375px iPhone screen the Gantt overflows horizontally with no pinch-to-zoom or swipe navigation, touch targets on bars are under 20px, and there is no simplified daily/weekly card view. A superintendent checking the schedule at 6am gets an unusable experience.
issues.prompt:
FILE: src/pages/Schedule.tsx
FILE: src/components/schedule/GanttChart.tsx
FILE: src/components/layout/ResponsiveShell.tsx

TASK: Add a mobile-optimized schedule view for screens under 768px.

CHANGES:
1. In src/pages/Schedule.tsx, detect viewport using a useMediaQuery('(max-width: 767px)') hook. On mobile, render a MobileScheduleView component instead of GanttChart.
2. Create src/components/schedule/MobileScheduleView.tsx: renders phases as a vertical card list grouped by week. Each card shows: phase name, status dot, progress bar (full width), start–end dates, assigned trade, float badge, and weather risk badge if applicable. Card is 48px min-height with 16px padding.
3. Add a horizontal date strip at the top with swipe-to-scroll (overflow-x: auto, snap scrolling). Current week is highlighted with an orange underline.
4. 'Today' button scrolls the date strip to the current week.
5. Tapping a phase card opens a bottom sheet detail view (not a modal) with full phase info and a 'Edit Dates' action for users with edit permission.
6. In GanttChart.tsx, set minimum bar height to 32px and minimum touch target wrapper to 44px for tablet views (768–1279px).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- MobileScheduleView renders correctly at 375px and 390px viewport widths.
- No horizontal overflow on mobile.
- Bottom sheet opens and closes with swipe-down gesture.
- Touch targets pass 44px minimum.
- Desktop Gantt is unaffected.

issues.title: Schedule page has no empty state, no loading skeleton, no error boundary
issues.description: src/pages/Schedule.tsx renders nothing if schedule_phases returns an empty array, shows a blank white area during fetch, and has no error boundary if the Gantt component throws. Design standards require skeleton loaders matching content shape, a specific empty state with CTA, and error boundaries on all page-level components.
issues.prompt:
FILE: src/pages/Schedule.tsx
FILE: src/components/schedule/GanttChart.tsx
FILE: src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx

TASK: Add loading skeleton, empty state, and error boundary to the Schedule page.

CHANGES:
1. In src/pages/Schedule.tsx, while isLoading=true render a skeleton: 5 metric card skeletons (pulse animation, matching card dimensions) + 8 Gantt row skeletons (varying widths 40-90%, height 32px, border-radius 4px, gray bg #E5E7EB, pulse 1.5s cycle).
2. When phases.length === 0 and !isLoading, render an empty state: Lucide 'CalendarX' icon (48px, #9CA3AF), heading 'No schedule activities yet', subtext 'Import your CPM schedule or add activities manually to get started.', primary button 'Import Schedule' and secondary button 'Add First Activity'.
3. Wrap GanttChart in src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx with a fallback that shows: 'Schedule could not be displayed', a technical detail (error.message) in a collapsed details tag, and a 'Reload' button that calls window.location.reload().
4. For API errors (isError=true), show an inline error card with the error message and a 'Retry' button that calls refetch().

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Skeleton matches the exact shape of the Gantt (rows + metric cards).
- Empty state shows the correct icon and both action buttons.
- ErrorBoundary catches a thrown error in GanttChart and shows the fallback without crashing the page.
- Retry button triggers a fresh API call.
- No 'No data found' generic messages anywhere.

issues.title: SchedulePhaseRow and mapped phase type are under-typed — missing construction-critical fields
issues.description: src/types/api.ts SchedulePhaseRow lacks float_days, is_critical_path, baseline_start_date, baseline_end_date, predecessor_ids, work_type, location, assigned_trade, and earned_value fields. The mapping in getSchedulePhases() uses ?? and || fallbacks masking data gaps. This prevents the Gantt, look-ahead, and AI engine from operating on the full domain model.
issues.prompt:
FILE: src/types/api.ts
FILE: src/api/endpoints/schedule.ts

TASK: Expand SchedulePhaseRow and the mapped phase type to cover the full construction schedule domain.

CHANGES:
1. In src/types/api.ts, extend SchedulePhaseRow with:
  - baseline_start_date: string | null
  - baseline_end_date: string | null
  - baseline_percent_complete: number | null
  - float_days: number | null
  - is_critical_path: boolean | null
  - is_milestone: boolean | null
  - predecessor_ids: string[] | null
  - work_type: 'indoor' | 'outdoor' | 'both' | null
  - location: string | null
  - assigned_trade: string | null
  - planned_labor_hours: number | null
  - actual_labor_hours: number | null
  - earned_value: number | null
2. Create a MappedSchedulePhase type in src/types/entities.ts with all non-null versions of the above plus computed fields: isOnCriticalPath (boolean), floatDays (number), scheduleVarianceDays (number).
3. Update getSchedulePhases() in src/api/endpoints/schedule.ts to map all new fields with correct fallbacks and return MappedSchedulePhase[].
4. Fix all downstream TypeScript errors.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- tsc --noEmit passes with zero new errors.
- No new 'any' types introduced.
- All new fields have explicit types (no implicit any from Supabase row).
- MappedSchedulePhase is exported and used by GanttChart and LookaheadBoard props.

issues.title: Schedule has no real-time updates — concurrent edits cause silent data divergence
issues.description: src/pages/Schedule.tsx fetches schedule_phases once on mount with no Supabase real-time subscription. If a PM updates a phase date on desktop while a superintendent views the schedule on mobile, the superintendent sees stale data with no notification. For a platform claiming to be the construction operating system, stale schedules cause coordination failures.
issues.prompt:
FILE: src/pages/Schedule.tsx
FILE: src/hooks/queries/realtime.ts
FILE: src/stores/scheduleStore.ts

TASK: Add real-time schedule phase updates via Supabase subscription.

CHANGES:
1. In src/hooks/queries/realtime.ts, add useRealtimeSchedulePhases(projectId: string) hook that:
  - Subscribes to supabase.channel('schedule_phases').on('postgres_changes', { event: '*', schema: 'public', table: 'schedule_phases', filter: `project_id=eq.${projectId}` }, handler)
  - On INSERT/UPDATE: calls queryClient.invalidateQueries(['schedule', projectId])
  - On DELETE: removes the phase from cache optimistically
  - Unsubscribes on unmount
2. In src/pages/Schedule.tsx, call useRealtimeSchedulePhases(projectId) alongside the existing query hook.
3. When an UPDATE event fires for a phase currently being edited (dirty state), show a non-blocking toast: 'This activity was updated by [user]. Reload to see changes.' with a 'Reload' action — do not auto-override unsaved edits.
4. Add a 'Live' indicator dot (8px green, pulse animation) in the Schedule page header when the subscription is active.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Phase updates from another session appear within 2 seconds without manual refresh.
- Conflict toast appears when an edited phase receives an external update.
- Subscription is cleaned up on component unmount (no memory leak).
- Live indicator is visible and accessible (aria-label='Live updates active').
- Hook is fully typed with no any.

issues.title: GanttChart is inaccessible — no keyboard navigation, no ARIA roles, screen reader unusable
issues.description: src/components/schedule/GanttChart.tsx renders schedule phases as visual bars with no role attributes, no aria-labels describing phase name/dates/progress, no keyboard focus management, and no skip-to-content for the timeline. Screen readers announce the Gantt as an unlabeled region. This fails WCAG 2.1 AA at the most basic level and blocks enterprise customers with ADA compliance requirements.
issues.prompt:
FILE: src/components/schedule/GanttChart.tsx

TASK: Make GanttChart keyboard-navigable and screen-reader accessible per WCAG 2.1 AA.

CHANGES:
1. Wrap the entire Gantt in a <div role='region' aria-label='Project Schedule Gantt Chart'>.
2. Add a visually hidden summary above the Gantt: 'Schedule contains N activities. X are on the critical path. Projected completion: [date].'
3. For each phase row, wrap in a <div role='row' aria-label='{name}: {startDate} to {endDate}, {progress}% complete, {floatDays} days float{critical ? ', critical path' : ''}' tabIndex={0}>.
4. Implement keyboard navigation: Tab moves between rows, Enter opens the detail panel, Left/Right arrows (when a row is focused) shift dates by 1 day and announce the new date via an aria-live='polite' region.
5. Add aria-valuenow, aria-valuemin=0, aria-valuemax=100, aria-label='{name} progress' to each progress bar element.
6. Ensure all interactive controls (drag handles, zoom buttons, baseline toggle) have aria-label attributes and are reachable by Tab.
7. Add a skip link at the top of Schedule.tsx: 'Skip to schedule activities' linking to the first phase row.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Axe DevTools reports zero critical or serious violations on the Schedule page.
- All phase rows are reachable and operable via keyboard alone.
- VoiceOver/NVDA announces phase name, dates, and progress when a row is focused.
- aria-live region announces date changes during keyboard navigation.
- No visual regressions on the desktop Gantt layout.

invented_features.title: Recovery Plan Generator
invented_features.description: When a critical-path activity slips, the AI analyzes remaining float across all paths, resource availability by trade, and historical productivity rates to generate 2-3 recovery scenarios (e.g., 'Add Saturday shift to concrete crew — recovers 4 days at $12K cost', 'Overlap MEP rough-in with framing — recovers 6 days, requires coordination meeting'). Each scenario shows cost, schedule impact, and risk level. PM can approve a scenario in one click and the schedule updates automatically. This is something Procore, ACC, and ALICE cannot do today in a unified workflow.

invented_features.title: Earned Value Live Dashboard
invented_features.description: A dedicated EV panel within the Schedule page showing BCWS, BCWP, ACWP, SPI, CPI, and EAC updated daily from actual cost and progress data. Includes an S-curve overlaying planned vs. earned vs. actual cost over time. Color-coded traffic light per phase. A CFO or bonding company can see the project's true performance at a glance — not just percent-complete but efficiency-adjusted forecast. No construction SaaS ships this natively in the schedule module today.

invented_features.title: Constraint Log integrated with Schedule
invented_features.description: A construction-specific constraint register linked directly to schedule activities. Each constraint (material delivery pending, permit not issued, RFI outstanding, sub not mobilized) blocks the linked activity with a visual chain icon on the Gantt bar. When the constraint is resolved (RFI closed, permit issued), the block is automatically lifted and the PM receives a notification: 'Constraint cleared: Electrical rough-in can now start.' Procore has no equivalent — constraints exist only in meeting notes today.

competitive_intel: The SiteSyncAI scheduling module is currently at parity with Fieldwire (basic Gantt, no financials) and significantly behind Procore (which has baseline comparison, resource loading, and look-ahead generation) and far behind ALICE Technologies (AI scenario optimization). The module lacks the two features that would make it categorically differentiated: the predictive delay engine and the recovery plan generator. Microsoft Project and Primavera P6 remain the gold standard for CPM scheduling among enterprise GCs — SiteSync must surpass their intelligence layer while matching their data fidelity to earn a switch. The integrations with primaveraP6.ts and msProject.ts exist in the services layer but are not surfaced in the Schedule UI, which is a missed quick-win. Competitors have no AI-native schedule intelligence. That gap is SiteSync's to own — but only if the AI predictions, baseline comparison, and recovery planning features are shipped.
summary: The Scheduling & Timeline module has a functional skeleton — a Gantt component, a look-ahead board, a schedule API endpoint, and integration stubs for P6 and MS Project — but it falls critically short of the construction domain depth and AI differentiation that define SiteSyncAI's north star. The three most damaging gaps are: (1) no baseline vs. actual comparison, which is a legal and contractual requirement for every GC running a CPM schedule; (2) no AI predictive delay engine, which is the single feature that would make a $500M GC switch overnight; and (3) no calculated schedule KPI metric cards, meaning the page opens to a visual chart with no at-a-glance health signal. Compounding these domain gaps are serious UX failures: the Gantt is read-only with no drag-to-adjust or dependency arrows, the mobile experience is completely unusable at 375px, and there are no loading skeletons, empty states, or error boundaries. The TypeScript type model for schedule phases is thin, missing float, earned value, work type, location, and baseline fields that the AI engine and look-ahead board need to function. Real-time subscription is absent, meaning concurrent edits cause silent data divergence. Accessibility is essentially zero — the Gantt renders as an unlabeled visual region with no keyboard navigation or ARIA roles. Immediate priorities are: baseline comparison view, AI risk panel wired to src/lib/predictions.ts, calculated KPI cards, mobile card view, and Gantt accessibility. Once those land, the Recovery Plan Generator and Earned Value Dashboard will position SiteSync as categorically ahead of Procore in schedule intelligence.
